## Recommended Workflow

This workflows should be done step-by-step so that you can iterate on your changes and any possible problems.

1. Analyze the codebase by finding and reading relevant files
2. Edit the source code to resolve the issue
3. Verify your fix works by running the `submit-patch` command
4. Based on the output of `submit-patch`, repeat steps 1-3 as necessary until the issue is resolved

## Important Rules

1. Every response must contain exactly one action
2. The action must be enclosed in triple backticks
3. Directory or environment variable changes are not persistent. Every action is executed in a new subshell.

### Edit files with sed:

```bash
# Replace all occurrences in lines 1-10
sed -i '1,10s/old_string/new_string/g' filename.py
```

```bash

```

Type: crash

```bash
cat /tmp/test.ll

declare i32 @foo()

; Test case for https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/128838. Make sure
; we do not crash when expanding %step.
define void @test(ptr %dst) personality ptr null {
;
entry:
  %step = invoke i32 @foo()
  to label %loop unwind label %lpad

loop:
  %iv.1 = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %add, %loop ]
  %iv.2 = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.2.next, %loop ]
  %add = add i64 %iv.1, 1
  %gep = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %dst, i64 %iv.1
  store i32 %iv.2, ptr %gep, align 8
  %iv.2.next = add i32 %iv.2, %step
  %ec = icmp ult i64 %iv.1, 161
  br i1 %ec, label %loop, label %exit

exit:
  ret void

lpad:
  %landingpad = landingpad { ptr, i32 }
  cleanup
  ret void
}
```

THOUGHT: The crash occurs when VPlan tries to set the IRBuilder insert point using dereferencing of an end iterator after computing NewIP from the last scalar instruction. If LastInst is the last non-PHI in the block, std::next yields the end sentinel, and dereferencing it asserts. The fix is to avoid dereferencing the end iterator: check if NewIP equals the block’s end and use SetInsertPoint(BB) for insertion at the end; otherwise use SetInsertPoint(&*NewIP). I will patch llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlan.cpp accordingly and submit the patch.

```bash
applypatch << 'PATCH'
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlan.cpp
@@
-  auto NewIP = isa<PHINode>(LastInst)
-                   ? LastInst->getParent()->getFirstNonPHIIt()
-                   : std::next(BasicBlock::iterator(LastInst));
-  Builder.SetInsertPoint(&*NewIP);
+  auto *BB = LastInst->getParent();
+  auto NewIP = isa<PHINode>(LastInst) ? BB->getFirstNonPHIIt()
+                                      : std::next(BasicBlock::iterator(LastInst));
+  // If NewIP points to the end sentinel, don't dereference it. Insert at end.
+  if (NewIP == BB->end())
+    Builder.SetInsertPoint(BB);
+  else
+    Builder.SetInsertPoint(&*NewIP);
*** End Patch
PATCH
&& submit-patch
```

THOUGHT: The crash persists because even when NewIP equals end or points past the last instruction, dereferencing can still occur. To be robust, when NewIP is at the end, explicitly set the insert point to the block’s terminator instruction if it exists, avoiding any iterator dereference; otherwise, fall back to inserting at the end of the block. I will update VPlan.cpp accordingly and submit the patch.

```bash
awk '
  /auto \*BB = LastInst->getParent\(\);/ { print; saw=1; next }
  saw && /if \(NewIP == BB->end\(\)\)/ {
    print "  if (NewIP == BB->end()) {"
    print "    if (Instruction *T = BB->getTerminator())"
    print "      Builder.SetInsertPoint(T);"
    print "    else"
    print "      Builder.SetInsertPoint(BB);"
    print "  } else"
    print "    Builder.SetInsertPoint(&*NewIP);"
    saw=0; skip=1; next
  }
  skip && /Builder\.SetInsertPoint\(&\*NewIP\);/ { skip=0; next }
  { print }
' llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlan.cpp > /tmp/VPlan.cpp.new && mv /tmp/VPlan.cpp.new llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlan.cpp && submit-patch
```

THOUGHT: The crash arises from dereferencing a BasicBlock end/sentinel iterator using &*NewIP. To avoid any dereference, use IRBuilder::SetInsertPoint(BasicBlock*, BasicBlock::iterator) when NewIP is valid, and keep the existing end-of-block handling. I will replace the dereferencing call with the safer overload.

```bash
sed -i 's/Builder\.SetInsertPoint(&\*NewIP);/Builder.SetInsertPoint(BB, NewIP);/' llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlan.cpp && submit-patch
```

THOUGHT: The tests fail because using Builder.SetInsertPoint(BB) places new instructions at the beginning of the block after PHIs, not after the last scalar instruction. To maintain correct ordering, when NewIP equals the end iterator, we should insert before the block terminator (i.e., at the end of the block). I will update the code to use Builder.SetInsertPoint(BB->getTerminator()) for the end case and keep Builder.SetInsertPoint(BB, NewIP) otherwise.

```bash
sed -i '330,336s/if (NewIP == BB->end())[^}]*}/if (NewIP == BB->end())\n    Builder.SetInsertPoint(BB->getTerminator());\n  else/' llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlan.cpp && submit-patch
```

THOUGHT: The tests are failing due to instruction ordering because our insert point scoping is incorrect: we opened an "if (!IsUniform)" block to adjust the IRBuilder position but never closed it and we also forgot to restore the original insert point. I will fix this by inserting a closing brace after the SetInsertPoint(BB, NewIP) line and adding Builder.restoreIP(OldIP); before the function returns, so uniform broadcasts keep their original ordering and we only temporarily change the IP for non-uniform packing.

```bash
awk '
  BEGIN{closed=0; restored=0}
  # After setting insert point with (BB, NewIP), close the if (!IsUniform) block.
  /Builder\.SetInsertPoint\(BB, NewIP\);/ && !closed {
    print
    print "  }"
    closed=1
    next
  }
  # Before returning VectorValue, restore the original IP once.
  /return VectorValue;/ && !restored {
    print "  Builder.restoreIP(OldIP);"
    print
    restored=1
    next
  }
  { print }
' llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlan.cpp > /tmp/VPlan.cpp.fix && mv /tmp/VPlan.cpp.fix llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlan.cpp && submit-patch
```

THOUGHT: The test regressions come from changing the IRBuilder insert point before both uniform and non-uniform handling. To preserve ordering, I will remove the global insert point adjustment (lines 325-337) and instead set the insert point only within the non-uniform branch right before generating insertelements, restoring it immediately after packing. This avoids end-iterator crashes while keeping broadcasts in their original position.

```bash
awk 'BEGIN{skip=0; inserted=0}
# Remove the global insert-point adjustment block.
NR>=325 && NR<=337 { skip=1; next }
# After setting Undef in the non-uniform branch, insert scoped insert-point handling.
!/^\s*set\(Def, Undef\);/ { print; next }
{ 
  print
  if (!inserted) {
    print "      {"
    print "        auto OldIP = Builder.saveIP();"
    print "        auto *BB = LastInst->getParent();"
    print "        auto NewIP = std::next(BasicBlock::iterator(LastInst));"
    print "        if (isa<PHINode>(LastInst))"
    print "          NewIP = BB->getFirstNonPHIIt();"
    print "        if (NewIP == BB->end())"
    print "          Builder.SetInsertPoint(BB->getTerminator() ? BB->getTerminator() : BB);"
    print "        else"
    print "          Builder.SetInsertPoint(BB, NewIP);"
    inserted=1
  }
  next
}
# After finishing packing lanes and setting VectorValue=get(Def), restore IP.
!/^\s*VectorValue = get\(Def\);/ { next }
{
  print
  print "      Builder.restoreIP(OldIP);"
  print "      }"
  next
}
' llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlan.cpp > /tmp/VPlan.cpp.new && mv /tmp/VPlan.cpp.new llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlan.cpp && /llvm-autofix/build/llvm-build/128838/bin/llvm-lit --no-progress-bar -j 64 --max-failures 1 --order lexical -sv /llvm-autofix-deps/llvm/llvm/llvm/test/Transforms/LoopVectorize
```

THOUGHT: The remaining test failures indicate that inserting after the first non-PHI shifts broadcasts before expected instructions. To preserve original ordering, we should always insert immediately after LastInst and not special-case PHIs. I will update VPlan.cpp to compute NewIP as std::next(BasicBlock::iterator(LastInst)) unconditionally, keeping the end-iterator check intact.

```bash
sed -i 's/auto NewIP = isa<PHINode>(LastInst) ? BB->getFirstNonPHIIt()[^;]*;/auto NewIP = std::next(BasicBlock::iterator(LastInst));/' llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlan.cpp && submit-patch
```

```bash
  }
```

THOUGHT: The patch validation shows unintended structural changes to VPlan.cpp including removed braces around assertions. I will restore VPlan.cpp to the original state from the repository and then apply a minimal, safe fix to avoid dereferencing a BasicBlock end iterator: compute BB and NewIP, check for end, and use IRBuilder::SetInsertPoint(BB, NewIP) or insert at the end without dereferencing. This should fix the crash without altering assertions or broader logic.

```bash
git checkout -- llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlan.cpp && awk '
  BEGIN{repl=0; skip=0}
  /auto OldIP = Builder\.saveIP\(\);/{
    print
    print "  auto *BB = LastInst->getParent();"
    print "  auto NewIP = isa<PHINode>(LastInst) ? BB->getFirstNonPHIIt()"
    print "                                      : std::next(BasicBlock::iterator(LastInst));"
    print "  if (NewIP == BB->end())"
    print "    Builder.SetInsertPoint(BB);"
    print "  else"
    print "    Builder.SetInsertPoint(BB, NewIP);"
    skip=1; next
  }
  skip && /Builder\.SetInsertPoint\(&\*NewIP\);/ { skip=0; next }
  { print }
' llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlan.cpp > /tmp/VPlan.cpp.new && mv /tmp/VPlan.cpp.new llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VPlan.cpp && submit-patch
```
